questions.q: Which broad region of India did the Satavahana dynasty primarily rule?
questions.explanation: The Satavahanas were the dominant power of the Deccan and parts of central India around the turn of the Common Era.

questions.q: Which Satavahana ruler is famous for resisting the Western Kshatrapas?
questions.explanation: Gautamiputra Satakarni is celebrated for defeating the Western Kshatrapas and reviving Satavahana power.

questions.q: The Satavahanas patronised which of the following religious traditions?
questions.explanation: Satavahana rulers supported both Buddhism and Brahmanical practices, funding monasteries while performing Vedic sacrifices.

questions.q: Around which period did the Satavahanas rule the Deccan?
options: Around the turn of the Common Era
questions.explanation: The Satavahanas flourished for several centuries around the turn of the Common Era, roughly the 1st century BCE to the 2nd century CE at their height.

questions.q: The Satavahanas were notable patrons of what economic activity?
questions.explanation: The Satavahanas actively encouraged inland and maritime trade, which brought prosperity to the Deccan.

questions.q: Which animal appears as the emblem associated with the Satavahana card in this deck?
questions.explanation: The Satavahanas are linked to the horse emblem, fitting their Deccan cavalry and the Ashvamedha (horse sacrifice) some rulers performed.

questions.q: The Western Kshatrapas, whom the Satavahanas fought, were rulers of what origin?
questions.explanation: The Western Kshatrapas were Saka (Indo-Scythian) rulers based in western India who clashed repeatedly with the Satavahanas.

questions.q: The Satavahana realm covered the Deccan and parts of which other broad area?
questions.explanation: Besides the Deccan, the Satavahanas controlled parts of central India during their long rule.

questions.q: Trade under the Satavahanas connected India with which distant empire by sea?
questions.explanation: Satavahana-era ports facilitated brisk maritime trade with the Roman world, attested by Roman coins found in the Deccan.

questions.q: The Satavahana dynasty is best described as a power of which broad era of Indian history?
questions.explanation: The Satavahanas rose in the post-Mauryan period, filling the political vacuum in the Deccan after the Mauryan decline.

questions.q: Gautamiputra Satakarni's achievements are recorded in an inscription set up by which royal woman?
questions.explanation: The Nashik prashasti (eulogy) recording Gautamiputra's deeds was issued by his mother, Gautami Balashri.

questions.q: Which language was predominantly used in Satavahana inscriptions and coins?
questions.explanation: The Satavahanas favoured Prakrit, often in the Brahmi script, for their official inscriptions and legends.

questions.q: The Western Kshatrapa ruler defeated by Gautamiputra Satakarni was known by which name?
questions.explanation: Gautamiputra Satakarni overran the Kshatrapa Nahapana, even restriking his silver coins, as hoards confirm.

questions.q: A famous Satavahana queen named in an early inscription at Naneghat was whose consort?
questions.explanation: Queen Naganika, named in the Naneghat inscription, was the consort of the early Satavahana ruler Satakarni I.

questions.q: The Satavahanas are sometimes referred to in the Puranas by which alternative dynastic name?
questions.explanation: Puranic texts often call the Satavahanas the Andhras or Andhrabhrityas, linking them to the Andhra region.

questions.q: Satavahana royal patronage helped fund Buddhist rock-cut caves at which group of sites?
questions.explanation: Satavahana-era donations supported Buddhist chaityas and viharas at western Deccan sites such as Karle, Nashik and Kanheri.

questions.q: Which Satavahana ruler is credited in tradition with composing the Prakrit poetry anthology Gatha Saptasati?
questions.explanation: The Satavahana king Hala is traditionally credited with compiling the Gatha Saptasati, a celebrated anthology of Prakrit verses.

questions.q: The Satavahanas issued coins prominently made of which metal in their trading economy?
options: Lead, along with silver and copper
questions.explanation: Satavahana coinage is distinctive for its widespread use of lead, alongside silver, copper and potin issues.

questions.q: Satavahana rulers asserted their Brahmanical legitimacy by performing which Vedic ritual?
questions.explanation: Several Satavahana kings, including Satakarni I, performed the Ashvamedha to proclaim sovereignty in the Vedic tradition.

questions.q: What feature of Satavahana naming reflected a matronymic (mother-based) custom?
questions.explanation: Satavahana kings often bore matronymics, such as Gautamiputra ('son of Gautami') and Vasishthiputra, naming the queen-mother's gotra.

questions.q: After Gautamiputra Satakarni, which son continued the dynasty and is named in the Nashik inscription?
questions.explanation: Gautamiputra was succeeded by his son Vasishthiputra Pulumavi, under whom the Nashik prashasti was inscribed.

questions.q: The traditional founder of the Satavahana dynasty is usually identified as whom?
questions.explanation: Simuka is generally regarded as the founder who established Satavahana rule, with later kings expanding the realm.

questions.q: The Western Kshatrapa Rudradaman I's Junagadh inscription mentions defeating a Satavahana ruler twice but sparing him because of which relationship?
questions.explanation: Rudradaman's Junagadh rock inscription says he twice defeated the Satavahana lord of Dakshinapatha but did not destroy him owing to close kinship (a marriage alliance).

questions.q: One important Satavahana administrative and trade centre in the eastern Deccan was Dhanyakataka, near which later Buddhist stupa site?
questions.explanation: Dhanyakataka, an eastern Satavahana capital, lay near Amaravati, whose great Buddhist stupa flourished under their patronage.

questions.q: Which western Deccan town served as a key Satavahana power centre in present-day Maharashtra?
questions.explanation: Pratishthana, modern Paithan on the Godavari, was a principal Satavahana capital in the western Deccan.

questions.q: The Nashik prashasti praises Gautamiputra Satakarni as the destroyer of which three foreign-origin groups?
questions.explanation: Gautami Balashri's inscription credits her son with crushing the Shakas, Yavanas (Greeks) and Pahlavas (Parthians).

questions.q: Satavahana-era maritime trade with Rome is described in which Greek navigational text of the 1st century CE?
options: The Periplus of the Erythraean Sea
questions.explanation: The Periplus of the Erythraean Sea details ports and goods of the Deccan coast, reflecting Satavahana-era Indo-Roman trade.

questions.q: Some Satavahana coins are notable in numismatics for bearing what unusual feature for the period?
options: A ship motif and bilingual legends
questions.explanation: Certain Satavahana coins display a two-masted ship, signalling sea trade, and some carry bilingual (Prakrit and Dravidian) legends.

questions.q: Which late Satavahana ruler, whose ship-type coins survive, is associated with the dynasty's final phase before its fragmentation?
questions.explanation: Yajna Sri Satakarni was a powerful late ruler whose ship-bearing coins are well known; the dynasty fragmented soon after his line.

questions.q: After the Satavahanas declined, the eastern part of their realm was inherited by which successor dynasty in Andhra?
questions.explanation: In the Krishna-Guntur region, the Ikshvakus of Vijayapuri succeeded the Satavahanas as a regional power.
